meaning: n.罩以面纱,面纱,面纱布料,帐幔
examples: I realized there was no fog veiling my window.
It is only hidden by the veiling and projecting power of Maya.
She is an instance of the first phase, which Cooke calls "self-veiling."
In other countries, where there is more choice in the matter, the reasons for veiling are more complex.
Mr Barak is still aloof and authoritarian, scarcely veiling his contempt for politics in general and for his own party in particular.
